method_note: Phase 09 refresh. The Zep benchmark audit re-read the refreshed export/extract artifacts and carried forward prior filing arbitration where the compared rows were unchanged.

field_mismatches.detail: The refreshed grouped April 14, 2014 proposal no longer includes New Mountain Capital. That brings the pipeline back in line with the filing language describing five actual IOI submitters.

Alex still has better explicit surface coverage for Party X / Party Y withdrawal rows. The refreshed pipeline chronology remains benchmark-clean on the proposal grouping, but the drop surface is still thinner than Alex's benchmark view.
The filing supports the board's February 26, 2015 decision to move forward with New Mountain Capital as the restart boundary, rather than Alex's February 19 IOI date.

pipeline_only.detail: Separate bidder-interest/restart framing around New Mountain Capital in February 2015 remains more granular than Alex's tracker rows.
The refreshed export still includes a separate bid-press-release row for April 8, 2015.
The export now carries both early board-sale framing rows, which is more detailed than Alex's single target-sale tracker row.
The grouped Party X / Party Y bidder-interest row has no direct Alex analogue.

alex_only.detail: Alex still tracks the March 27 process-letter milestone and related informal-round framing as benchmark rows.
Alex still carries explicit Party X and Party Y withdrawal rows that the refreshed pipeline does not surface separately.
Alex keeps the February 27, 2015 exclusivity row, which remains outside the pipeline event vocabulary.

aggregate_rows.detail: Alex's aggregate NDA cohort row remains a soft benchmark match rather than a 1:1 atomic match.
Alex's '5 parties, 4F and 1S' proposal row now aligns better with the refreshed grouped pipeline proposal after the NMC fix.
Alex's aggregate dropout framing remains more explicit than the refreshed pipeline surface.

export_regressions.detail: The refreshed Zep coverage notes still cite drop ids evt_009 and evt_011 even though those ids are not materialized in the current events array. This did not block export, but it remains a reconciliation attention item.

summary.verdict: The Phase 09 Zep rerun fixed the New Mountain Capital contamination in the grouped 2014 proposal cohort, but the refreshed benchmark still flags thinner dropout coverage and a remaining coverage-note integrity concern.
